title: Counter-Height Fancy X Farmhouse Table
summary: The counter-height version of this catalog's Fancy X farmhouse table — the same layered 2x4 X-leg construction and 45° compound tips, raised to counter-stool height with a 1x6 plank top.
description:
This shares the same layered 2x4 X-leg construction as this catalog's dining-height Fancy X Farmhouse Table — 4x4 posts with 1x4 and 2x4 pieces layered around them, and matching 45°-beveled X crosses meeting at the center — but built taller for counter stools and topped in narrower 1x6 boards instead of a wide 2x10 plank. The height and top material are genuine differences, not a copy-paste of the dining version.

Build the two leg sets first, each combining a 4x4 post with layered 1x4 and 2x4 pieces plus the angled X-brace pieces — dry-fit the compound-angle pieces before committing to glue, since the 45° bevels on multiple pieces compound any small error. The center assembly ties the two leg sets together with its own angled bracing.

The tabletop is five 1x6 boards edge to edge, trimmed on both long edges with 1x2 boards **cut to fit** rather than a fixed length — measure the assembled top before cutting these last two pieces.

materials.name: Primer and paint, or stain and poly

cutList.note: Long point to long point, both ends cut at 45° bevel, ends not parallel.
Long point to long point, both ends cut at 45° bevel, ends not parallel.
Long point to short point, both ends cut at 45° bevel, ends parallel.
Longest point measurement, one end cut at 45° bevel.

cutList.part: Top edge trim, cut to fit
cutList.note: Measure and cut to fit the assembled top.

steps.title: Build the two leg sets
steps.body: Dry-fit the compound-angle X brace pieces on each 4x4 post before gluing, then assemble both leg sets with the uprights and blocking.

steps.title: Build and attach the center assembly
steps.body: Assemble the center stretchers, brace, and angled legs, then join the two leg sets together.

steps.title: Build the top
steps.body: Join the five tabletop boards edge to edge, then measure and cut the last two edge trim pieces to fit before attaching all four.

steps.title: Attach the top to the base
steps.body: Attach the finished top to the leg assembly.

steps.title: Finish
steps.body: Sand all surfaces and apply primer and paint, or stain and poly.
materials: Primer and paint, or stain and poly
